237.reason: [Error: When encountering a single '&', the code incorrectly returns a token with type AND and value "&&" instead of recording the error and not outputting a token. According to the problem, a single '&' should be an error (type a), and the program should output the error line number and error code, not generate a token. However, the code still returns a token, which will be added to the token list and output, causing incorrect output for error cases.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When encountering a single '&', the code incorrectly returns a token with type AND and value "&&" instead of recording the error and not outputting a token. According to the problem, a single '&' should be an error (type a), and the program should output the error line number and error code, not generate a token. However, the code still returns a token, which will be added to the token list and output, causing incorrect output for error cases.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Potential StringIndexOutOfBoundsException if pos is at end of string; also, when next char is not '&', the token value "&&" is incorrect but error is recorded
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing length check: pos may be out of bounds
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing boundary check for pos before accessing string.charAt(pos), which causes StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when '&' is the last character in the input.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Missing bounds check for pos before accessing string.charAt(pos), causing StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when '&' appears at the end of the input]

248.reason: [Error: Same issue as with '&'. When encountering a single '|', the code incorrectly returns a token with type OR and value "||" instead of only recording the error. This will cause the program to output both the error and a token, which is incorrect.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Same issue as with '&'. When encountering a single '|', the code incorrectly returns a token with type OR and value "||" instead of only recording the error. This will cause the program to output both the error and a token, which is incorrect.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Same issue as above: possible out-of-bounds and incorrect token value for single '|'
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing length check: pos may be out of bounds
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing boundary check for pos before accessing string.charAt(pos), which causes StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when '|' is the last character in the input.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Missing bounds check for pos before accessing string.charAt(pos), causing StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when '|' appears at the end of the input]

239.reason: [Error: When a single '&' is encountered, the program should output an error with category code 'a' and not output any token. However, this code returns a token with LexType.AND and value "&&", which is incorrect. The error should be recorded and the token should not be added to the token list.]
